notes: Spec asserts: ready → working → ready and state never enters waiting. The recipe drives a single auto-executed Bash call ('echo hello') followed by a terminal reply. The bash_tool_use phase is a tool-use lifecycle marker (kind:'tool_use') that anchors the no-waiting invariant on the working span. Permission gating is out-of-scope here — auto-approved tools (or pre-approved settings) keep PermissionPending unset, so rule 0 cannot fire and waiting is unreachable. If the recording ever flips to waiting during this scenario, that means the Bash tool tripped a gate (different feature: Tool gate via permission prompt) and the recipe must be tightened.

text: Turn completes back to ready; the Bash tool_use ran auto-approved and state never entered waiting (spec bullet 2)
